1→#!/usr/bin/env python
     2→"""Main CLI entry point for Maimai Assistant."""
     3→
     4→import sys
     5→import asyncio
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→
     8→import click
     9→from rich.console import Console
    10→from rich.table import Table
    11→
    12→# Add src to path
    13→sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent))
    14→
    15→from src.utils.logger import setup_logger
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config
    17→from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter
    18→from src.utils.delays import reset_session as reset_delay_session
    19→from src.exceptions.errors import MaimaiAssistantError
    20→
    21→console = Console()
    22→
    23→
    24→def run_async(coro):
    25→    """Run async function in sync context."""
    26→    return asyncio.run(coro)
    27→
    28→
    29→def _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx):
    30→    """Apply CDP endpoint override from CLI --cdp flag to browser manager."""
    31→    cdp_endpoint = ctx.obj.get('cdp_endpoint') if ctx.obj else None
    32→    if cdp_endpoint:
    33→        from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    34→        get_browser_manager().set_cdp_endpoint(cdp_endpoint)
    35→
    36→
    37→@click.group()
    38→@click.version_option(version='0.1.0')
    39→@click.option('--cdp', 'cdp_endpoint', default=None, envvar='MAIMAI_CDP_ENDPOINT',
    40→              help='CDP endpoint URL (default: http://localhost:9222)')
    41→@click.pass_context
    42→def cli(ctx, cdp_endpoint):
    43→    """
    44→    Maimai Semi-Automated Recruitment Assistant
    45→
    46→    Connects to your Chrome browser via CDP. Start Chrome with:
    47→
    48→        /Applications/Google\\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222
    49→
    50→    Then log in to Maimai manually before running commands.
    51→    """
    52→    try:
    53→        # Initialize configuration and logging
    54→        setup_logger()
    55→        ctx.ensure_object(dict)
    56→        ctx.obj['cdp_endpoint'] = cdp_endpoint
    57→    except Exception as e:
    58→        console.print(f"[red]Failed to initialize application: {e}[/red]")
    59→        sys.exit(1)
    60→
    61→
    62→@cli.command()
    63→@click.pass_context
    64→def login(ctx):
    65→    """
    66→    Verify login status via CDP connection.
    67→
    68→    Connects to your Chrome browser and checks if you're logged in to Maimai.
    69→    If not, waits for you to log in manually in the browser.
    70→
    71→    First, start Chrome with remote debugging:
    72→
    73→        /Applications/Google\\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\\ Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222
    74→
    75→    Then log in to Maimai in that Chrome window.
    76→    """
    77→    from src.modules.auth import login as do_login
    78→    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
    79→
    80→    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)
    81→
    82→    async def _login():
    83→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    84→        try:
    85→            success = await do_login(browser)
    86→            if success:
    87→                console.print("[green]Login verified![/green]")
    88→            else:
    89→                console.print("[red]Login failed or timed out.[/red]")
    90→                sys.exit(1)
    91→        finally:
    92→            await browser.close()
    93→
    94→    run_async(_login())
    95→
    96→
    97→@cli.command()
    98→@click.option('--keywords', '-k', required=True, multiple=True, help='Search keywords (can specify multiple times)')
    99→@click.option('--company', '-c', multiple=True, help='Filter by company name (can specify multiple)')
   100→@click.option('--location', '-l', help='Filter by location')
   101→@click.option('--max-pages', '-p', type=int, default=1, help='Maximum pages to scrape per keyword')
   102→@click.option('--mode', '-m', type=click.Choice(['search_center', 'enterprise']), default='search_center',
   103→              help='Search mode: search_center (default) or enterprise')
   104→@click.option('--export', '-e', help='Export results to file (Excel/CSV)')
   105→@click.pass_context
   106→def search(ctx, keywords, company, location, max_pages, mode, export):
   107→    """
   108→    Search for candidates with specified criteria.
   109→
   110→    Supports multiple keywords - each will be searched separately and results merged.
   111→
   112→    Use --mode enterprise to search via the enterprise recruitment tab.
   113→
   114→    Examples:\n
   115→        maimai search -k "高级前端工程师" -c "阿里巴巴" --max-pages 3\n
   116→        maimai search -k "前端工程师" -k "React开发" -k "Vue工程师" -p 2\n
   117→        maimai search -k "研发" -m enterprise
   118→    """
   119→    from src.modules.export import export_candidates
   120→    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
   121→    from src.services.pipeline import search_all_keywords, search_all_keywords_enterprise
   122→
   123→    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)
   124→
   125→    async def _search():
   126→        reset_delay_session()
   127→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   128→        try:
   129→            if mode == 'enterprise':
   130→                all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   131→                    browser, keywords, company, max_pages
   132→                )
   133→            else:
   134→                all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
   135→                    browser, keywords, company, location, max_pages
   136→                )
   137→
   138→            # Display combined results
   139→            if all_candidates:
   140→                table = Table(title=f"Search Results ({len(all_candidates)} candidates total)")
   141→                table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
   142→                table.add_column("Title", style="green")
   143→                table.add_column("Company", style="yellow")
   144→                table.add_column("Location", style="blue")
   145→                table.add_column("ID", style="dim")
   146→
   147→                for c in all_candidates[:30]:  # Show first 30
   148→                    table.add_row(c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or '', c.maimai_id)
   149→
   150→                console.print(table)
   151→
   152→                if len(all_candidates) > 30:
   153→                    console.print(f"[dim]... and {len(all_candidates) - 30} more[/dim]")
   154→            else:
   155→                console.print("[yellow]No candidates found for any keyword.[/yellow]")
   156→
   157→            # Export if requested
   158→            if export and all_candidates:
   159→                fmt = 'csv' if export.endswith('.csv') else 'excel'
   160→                export_candidates(format=fmt, output_path=export)
   161→
   162→        finally:
   163→            await browser.close()
   164→
   165→    run_async(_search())

   194→@cli.command()
   195→@click.argument('maimai_id')
   196→@click.option('--template', '-t', required=True, help='Message template name')

   198→@click.option('--var', '-v', multiple=True, help='Template variable (format: key=value)')
   199→@click.option('--interactive', '-i', is_flag=True, help='Enable interactive selector discovery when auto-detection fails')
   200→@click.pass_context
   201→def message(ctx, maimai_id, template, preview, var, interactive):
   202→    """
   203→    Send message to a candidate (requires confirmation).
   204→
   205→    Example:
   206→        maimai message abc123 -t greeting -v name=张三 -v recruiter_name=李四
   207→        maimai message abc123 -t follow_up --preview
   208→        maimai message abc123 -t greeting -i
   209→    """
   210→    from src.modules.message import send_message, preview_message
   211→    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
   212→
   213→    # Parse variables
   214→    variables = _parse_variables(var)
   215→
   216→    if preview:
   217→        # Just preview, no send
   218→        preview_message(template, variables)
   219→        return
   220→
   221→    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)
   222→
   223→    async def _message():
   224→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   225→        try:
   226→            discovered_selectors = None
   227→            if interactive:
   228→                from src.modules.selector_discovery import DiscoveredSelectors
   229→                discovered_selectors = DiscoveredSelectors()
   230→
   231→            success = await send_message(
   232→                maimai_id=maimai_id,
   233→                template_name=template,
   234→                variables=variables,
   235→                browser=browser,
   236→                interactive=interactive,
   237→                discovered_selectors=discovered_selectors,
   238→            )
   239→            if not success:
   240→                sys.exit(1)
   241→        finally:
   242→            await browser.close()
   243→

   247→@cli.command()
   248→@click.option('--keywords', '-k', multiple=True, help='Search keywords (can specify multiple)')
   249→@click.option('--template', '-t', help='Message template name')
   250→@click.option('--var', '-v', multiple=True, help='Template variable (format: key=value)')
   251→@click.option('--company', '-c', multiple=True, help='Filter by company name (can specify multiple)')
   252→@click.option('--location', '-l', help='Filter by location')
   253→@click.option('--max-pages', '-p', type=int, default=1, help='Max pages to scrape per keyword')
   254→@click.option('--mode', '-m', type=click.Choice(['search_center', 'enterprise']), default='search_center',
   255→              help='Search mode: search_center (default) or enterprise')
   256→@click.option('--dry-run', is_flag=True, help='Search only, do not send messages')
   257→@click.option('--skip-messaged/--no-skip-messaged', default=True, help='Skip already messaged candidates')
   258→@click.option('--batch-pages', '-b', type=int, default=0, help='Send messages every N pages (0 = send after all pages)')
   259→@click.option('--interactive', '-i', is_flag=True, help='Enable interactive selector discovery when auto-detection fails')
   260→@click.option('--resume', is_flag=True, help='Resume from last checkpoint')
   261→@click.pass_context
   262→def pipeline(ctx, keywords, template, var, company, location, max_pages, mode, dry_run, skip_messaged, batch_pages, interactive, resume):
   263→    """
   264→    Search candidates and batch send messages in one command.
   265→
   266→    Combines multi-keyword search with batch messaging:
   267→    search → preview → confirm → send.
   268→
   269→    Use --mode enterprise for the enterprise recruitment path with inline messaging.
   270→
   271→    Use --batch-pages to interleave search and messaging every N pages.
   272→    Example: -p 10 --batch-pages 2 searches pages 1-2, sends messages,
   273→    then pages 3-4, sends messages, etc.
   274→
   275→    Use --resume to continue from the last interrupted pipeline run.
   276→
   277→    Examples:\n
   278→        maimai pipeline -k "前端工程师" -t greeting -v "recruiter_name=张三" -p 2\n
   279→        maimai pipeline -k "前端" -k "后端" -t greeting -v "recruiter_name=张三" --dry-run\n
   280→        maimai pipeline -k "前端" -t greeting -p 10 --batch-pages 2\n
   281→        maimai pipeline -k "前端" -t greeting -p 1 -i\n
   282→        maimai pipeline -k "研发" -t greeting -m enterprise -p 3\n
   283→        maimai pipeline --resume
   284→    """
   285→    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
   286→    from src.utils.checkpoint import CheckpointManager
   287→    from src.services.pipeline import run_pipeline_default, run_pipeline_batched, SearchMode
   288→
   289→    # Handle resume mode
   290→    if resume:
   291→        ckpt = CheckpointManager.load_latest()
   292→        if ckpt is None:
   293→            console.print("[red]No checkpoint found to resume from.[/red]")
   294→            return
   295→
   296→        from rich.panel import Panel
   297→        console.print(Panel(ckpt.summary, title="Resuming Pipeline", border_style="cyan"))
   298→
   299→        p = ckpt.params
   300→        keywords = tuple(p.get('keywords', []))
   301→        template = p.get('template', template)
   302→        variables = p.get('variables', {})
   303→        company = tuple(p.get('company', []))
   304→        location = p.get('location', location)
   305→        max_pages = p.get('max_pages', max_pages)
   306→        batch_pages = p.get('batch_pages', batch_pages)
   307→        skip_messaged = p.get('skip_messaged', skip_messaged)
   308→        interactive = p.get('interactive', interactive)
   309→        mode = p.get('mode', mode)
   310→    else:
   311→        ckpt = None
   312→        variables = _parse_variables(var)
   313→
   314→    if not keywords or not template:
   315→        console.print("[red]--keywords (-k) and --template (-t) are required (or use --resume).[/red]")
   316→        return
   317→
   318→    # Resolve SearchMode enum
   319→    search_mode = SearchMode(mode)
   320→
   321→    if search_mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE and batch_pages > 0:
   322→        console.print("[yellow]Note: --batch-pages with enterprise mode uses infinite scroll; "
   323→                      "each batch opens a fresh enterprise search. Deduplication prevents re-messaging.[/yellow]")
   324→
   325→    _apply_cdp_endpoint(ctx)
   326→
   327→    async def _pipeline():
   328→        from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
   329→
   330→        reset_delay_session()
   331→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   332→        try:
   333→            await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   334→
   335→            if batch_pages > 0:
   336→                await run_pipeline_batched(
   337→                    browser, keywords, company, location,
   338→                    max_pages, batch_pages, template, variables,
   339→                    dry_run, skip_messaged, interactive, ckpt,
   340→                    mode=search_mode,
   341→                )
   342→            else:
   343→                await run_pipeline_default(
   344→                    browser, keywords, company, location,
   345→                    max_pages, template, variables,
   346→                    dry_run, skip_messaged, interactive,
   347→                    mode=search_mode,
   348→                )
   349→        finally:
   350→            await browser.close()
   351→
   352→    run_async(_pipeline())
   353→

   478→
   479→def _parse_variables(var: tuple) -> dict:
   480→    """Parse key=value variable pairs from CLI option."""
   481→    variables = {}
   482→    for v in var:
   483→        if '=' in v:
   484→            key, value = v.split('=', 1)
   485→            variables[key.strip()] = value.strip()
   486→    return variables
   487→
   488→
   489→def main():
   490→    """Main entry point."""
   491→    try:
   492→        cli()
   493→    except MaimaiAssistantError as e:
   494→        console.print(f"[red]Error: {e}[/red]")
   495→        sys.exit(1)
   496→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   497→        console.print("\n[yellow]Operation cancelled by user[/yellow]")
   498→        sys.exit(0)
   499→    except Exception as e:
   500→        console.print(f"[red]Unexpected error: {e}[/red]")
   501→        sys.exit(1)
   502→
   503→
   504→if __name__ == '__main__':
   505→    main()

1→"""Browser manager for Playwright automation.
     2→
     3→Provides a singleton browser manager that connects to an existing Chrome
     4→instance via CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol). Supports multi-tab management
     5→for workflows that open new tabs (e.g., enterprise recruitment).
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→from pathlib import Path
    10→from typing import List, Optional
    11→from datetime import datetime
    12→
    13→from patchright.async_api import async_playwright, Browser, BrowserContext, Page, Playwright
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    17→from src.core.anti_detection import apply_stealth_scripts
    18→from src.core.session import SessionManager, get_session_manager
    19→from src.exceptions.errors import BrowserError
    20→
    21→
    22→class BrowserManager:
    23→    """
    24→    Singleton browser manager for Maimai automation.
    25→
    26→    Connects to a user-managed Chrome instance via CDP endpoint.
    27→    The user starts Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and logs in
    28→    manually. This manager connects/disconnects without closing Chrome.
    29→    """
    30→
    31→    _instance: Optional['BrowserManager'] = None
    32→    _lock: Optional[asyncio.Lock] = None
    33→
    34→    def __new__(cls, **kwargs):
    35→        if cls._instance is None:
    36→            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
    37→            cls._instance._initialized = False
    38→        return cls._instance
    39→
    40→    def __init__(
    41→        self,
    42→        config: Optional[Config] = None,
    43→        session_manager: Optional[SessionManager] = None,
    44→    ):
    45→        if self._initialized:
    46→            return
    47→
    48→        self._playwright: Optional[Playwright] = None
    49→        self._browser: Optional[Browser] = None
    50→        self._context: Optional[BrowserContext] = None
    51→        self._page: Optional[Page] = None
    52→        self._primary_page: Optional[Page] = None  # Original tab to return to
    53→        self._opened_tabs: List[Page] = []  # Tabs opened during our session
    54→        self._session_manager: SessionManager = session_manager or get_session_manager()
    55→        self._config: Config = config or get_config()
    56→        self._cdp_endpoint: Optional[str] = None
    57→        self._owned_page: bool = False  # True if we created the page (vs reusing existing)
    58→        self._initialized = True
    59→
    60→    @property
    61→    def is_running(self) -> bool:
    62→        """Check if browser is currently connected."""
    63→        return self._browser is not None and self._browser.is_connected()
    64→
    65→    @property
    66→    def page(self) -> Optional[Page]:
    67→        """Get current page instance."""
    68→        return self._page
    69→
    70→    @property
    71→    def context(self) -> Optional[BrowserContext]:
    72→        """Get current browser context."""
    73→        return self._context
    74→
    75→    @classmethod
    76→    def _ensure_lock(cls):
    77→        """Lazily create the asyncio.Lock inside a running event loop."""
    78→        if cls._lock is None:
    79→            cls._lock = asyncio.Lock()
    80→
    81→    def set_cdp_endpoint(self, endpoint: str) -> None:
    82→        """Override CDP endpoint (e.g. from CLI --cdp flag)."""
    83→        self._cdp_endpoint = endpoint
    84→
    85→    async def start(self, headless: Optional[bool] = None) -> Page:
    86→        """
    87→        Connect to Chrome via CDP and return a page.
    88→
    89→        The headless parameter is accepted for API compatibility but ignored
    90→        in CDP mode (the user's Chrome is always visible).
    91→
    92→        Returns:
    93→            Configured Playwright page instance
    94→
    95→        Raises:
    96→            BrowserError: If connection fails
    97→        """
    98→        self._ensure_lock()
    99→        async with self._lock:
   100→            if self.is_running:
   101→                logger.debug("Browser already connected, returning existing page")
   102→                return self._page
   103→
   104→            try:
   105→                # Determine CDP endpoint
   106→                endpoint = self._cdp_endpoint or self._config.get(
   107→                    'browser.cdp_endpoint', 'http://localhost:9222'
   108→                )
   109→                timeout = self._config.get('browser.timeout', 30000)
   110→
   111→                logger.info(f"Connecting to Chrome via CDP: {endpoint}")
   112→
   113→                # Start Playwright
   114→                self._playwright = await async_playwright().start()
   115→
   116→                # Connect to existing Chrome via CDP
   117→                self._browser = await self._playwright.chromium.connect_over_cdp(endpoint)
   118→
   119→                # Get the default context (the user's browser context with cookies)
   120→                contexts = self._browser.contexts
   121→                if contexts:
   122→                    self._context = contexts[0]
   123→                else:
   124→                    # Fallback: create a new context (shouldn't happen with CDP)
   125→                    logger.warning("No existing context found, creating new one")
   126→                    self._context = await self._browser.new_context()
   127→
   128→                # Set default timeout
   129→                self._context.set_default_timeout(timeout)
   130→
   131→                # Reuse existing page or create one
   132→                pages = self._context.pages
   133→                if pages:
   134→                    self._page = pages[0]
   135→                    self._owned_page = False
   136→                    logger.info(f"Reusing existing tab: {self._page.url}")
   137→                else:
   138→                    self._page = await self._context.new_page()
   139→                    self._owned_page = True
   140→                    logger.info("Created new tab")
   141→
   142→                # Remember the primary tab
   143→                self._primary_page = self._page
   144→                self._opened_tabs = []
   145→
   146→                # Register event listener to auto-apply stealth scripts to new tabs
   147→                self._context.on("page", self._on_new_page)
   148→
   149→                # Apply anti-detection stealth scripts
   150→                await apply_stealth_scripts(self._page)
   151→
   152→                logger.info("Connected to Chrome via CDP successfully")
   153→                return self._page
   154→
   155→            except Exception as e:
   156→                logger.error(f"Failed to connect to Chrome via CDP: {e}")
   157→                await self.close()
   158→                raise BrowserError(
   159→                    f"Failed to connect to Chrome via CDP at {endpoint}. "
   160→                    f"Make sure Chrome is running with --remote-debugging-port=9222. "
   161→                    f"Error: {e}"
   162→                )
   163→
   164→    async def close(self) -> None:
   165→        """Disconnect from Chrome via CDP. Does NOT close the user's Chrome."""
   166→        self._ensure_lock()
   167→        async with self._lock:
   168→            try:
   169→                # Close tabs we opened during the session
   170→                for tab in self._opened_tabs:
   171→                    try:
   172→                        if not tab.is_closed():
   173→                            await tab.close()
   174→                    except Exception:
   175→                        pass
   176→                self._opened_tabs = []
   177→
   178→                # Only close pages we created, not the user's existing tabs
   179→                if self._page and self._owned_page:
   180→                    try:
   181→                        await self._page.close()
   182→                    except Exception:
   183→                        pass
   184→                self._page = None
   185→                self._primary_page = None
   186→                self._owned_page = False
   187→
   188→                # Don't close the context — it belongs to the user's Chrome
   189→                self._context = None
   190→
   191→                # browser.close() in CDP mode just disconnects, doesn't kill Chrome
   192→                if self._browser:
   193→                    await self._browser.close()
   194→                    self._browser = None
   195→
   196→                if self._playwright:
   197→                    await self._playwright.stop()
   198→                    self._playwright = None
   199→
   200→                logger.info("Disconnected from Chrome (browser stays open)")
   201→
   202→            except Exception as e:
   203→                logger.error(f"Error disconnecting from Chrome: {e}")
   204→
   205→    async def _on_new_page(self, page: Page) -> None:
   206→        """Callback for context 'page' event — apply stealth to new tabs."""
   207→        try:
   208→            await apply_stealth_scripts(page)
   209→            logger.debug(f"Applied stealth scripts to new tab: {page.url}")
   210→        except Exception as e:
   211→            logger.warning(f"Failed to apply stealth to new tab: {e}")
   212→
   213→    async def wait_for_new_tab(self, timeout: int = 10000) -> Page:
   214→        """Wait for a click-triggered new tab, apply stealth, and switch to it.
   215→
   216→        Call this right after performing an action that opens a new tab
   217→        (e.g., clicking a link with target=_blank).
   218→
   219→        Args:
   220→            timeout: Max wait time in milliseconds
   221→
   222→        Returns:
   223→            The new Page instance (also set as the active page)
   224→
   225→        Raises:
   226→            BrowserError: If no new tab appears within timeout
   227→        """
   228→        if not self._context:
   229→            raise BrowserError("No active context for wait_for_new_tab")
   230→
   231→        try:
   232→            new_page = await self._context.wait_for_event(
   233→                "page", timeout=timeout
   234→            )
   235→            await new_page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded")
   236→            self._opened_tabs.append(new_page)
   237→            self._page = new_page
   238→            logger.info(f"New tab captured: {new_page.url}")
   239→            return new_page
   240→        except Exception as e:
   241→            raise BrowserError(f"No new tab opened within {timeout}ms: {e}")
   242→
   243→    def switch_to_latest_tab(self) -> Page:
   244→        """Switch _page to the most recently opened tab in the context.
   245→
   246→        Returns:
   247→            The latest Page instance
   248→        """
   249→        if not self._context or not self._context.pages:
   250→            raise BrowserError("No tabs available")
   251→
   252→        self._page = self._context.pages[-1]
   253→        logger.debug(f"Switched to latest tab: {self._page.url}")
   254→        return self._page
   255→
   256→    def switch_to_primary_tab(self) -> Page:
   257→        """Switch back to the original (primary) tab.
   258→
   259→        Returns:
   260→            The primary Page instance
   261→        """
   262→        if not self._primary_page or self._primary_page.is_closed():
   263→            raise BrowserError("Primary tab is not available")
   264→
   265→        self._page = self._primary_page
   266→        logger.debug(f"Switched to primary tab: {self._page.url}")
   267→        return self._page
   268→
   269→    async def close_current_tab(self) -> Page:
   270→        """Close the current active tab and switch to the previous one.
   271→
   272→        Will not close the primary tab. After closing, switches to the
   273→        most recent remaining tab or falls back to the primary tab.
   274→
   275→        Returns:
   276→            The page that became active after closing
   277→
   278→        Raises:
   279→            BrowserError: If trying to close the primary tab
   280→        """
   281→        if not self._page:
   282→            raise BrowserError("No active page to close")
   283→
   284→        if self._page == self._primary_page:
   285→            raise BrowserError("Cannot close the primary tab")
   286→
   287→        closing = self._page
   288→        try:
   289→            # Remove from opened_tabs tracking
   290→            if closing in self._opened_tabs:
   291→                self._opened_tabs.remove(closing)
   292→            await closing.close()
   293→        except Exception as e:
   294→            logger.warning(f"Error closing tab: {e}")
   295→
   296→        # Switch to previous tab
   297→        if self._context and self._context.pages:
   298→            self._page = self._context.pages[-1]
   299→        else:
   300→            self._page = self._primary_page
   301→
   302→        logger.debug(f"Closed tab, switched to: {self._page.url if self._page else 'none'}")
   303→        return self._page
   304→
   305→    @property
   306→    def tab_count(self) -> int:
   307→        """Return the number of open tabs in the context."""
   308→        if not self._context:
   309→            return 0
   310→        return len(self._context.pages)
   311→
   312→    async def save_session(self) -> None:
   313→        """Save current session to disk (backup of cookies)."""
   314→        if self._context:
   315→            await self._session_manager.save(self._context)
   316→        else:
   317→            logger.warning("No active context to save session from")
   318→
   319→    async def screenshot(self, name: str = None) -> str:
   320→        """
   321→        Take a screenshot of current page.
   322→
   323→        Args:
   324→            name: Screenshot name (timestamp used if None)
   325→
   326→        Returns:
   327→            Path to saved screenshot
   328→        """
   329→        if not self._page:
   330→            raise BrowserError("No active page for screenshot")
   331→
   332→        screenshot_dir = Path(self._config.get('data.screenshot_dir', 'data/screenshots'))
   333→        screenshot_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   334→
   335→        if name is None:
   336→            name = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')
   337→
   338→        filepath = screenshot_dir / f"{name}.png"
   339→
   340→        await self._page.screenshot(path=str(filepath), full_page=True)
   341→        logger.info(f"Screenshot saved: {filepath}")
   342→
   343→        return str(filepath)
   344→
   345→
   346→
   347→# Global browser manager instance
   348→_browser_manager: Optional[BrowserManager] = None
   349→
   350→
   351→def get_browser_manager() -> BrowserManager:
   352→    """Get global browser manager instance."""
   353→    global _browser_manager
   354→    if _browser_manager is None:
   355→        _browser_manager = BrowserManager()
   356→    return _browser_manager
   357→
   358→
   359→async def ensure_browser() -> Page:
   360→    """
   361→    Ensure browser is connected and return page.
   362→
   363→    Convenience function for modules that need a browser.
   364→
   365→    Returns:
   366→        Active page instance
   367→    """
   368→    manager = get_browser_manager()
   369→    if not manager.is_running:
   370→        return await manager.start()
   371→    return manager.page
   372→

1→"""Session management for persistent browser sessions.
     2→
     3→Handles saving and loading browser sessions (cookies, localStorage)
     4→to enable resuming without re-login.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import json
     8→import os
     9→import stat
    10→from pathlib import Path
    11→from datetime import datetime, timedelta
    12→from typing import Optional
    13→
    14→from cryptography.fernet import Fernet
    15→from patchright.async_api import Page, BrowserContext
    16→from loguru import logger
    17→
    18→from src.utils.config import get_config
    19→from src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError
    20→
    21→
    22→class SessionManager:
    23→    """Manages browser session persistence."""
    24→
    25→    def __init__(self, session_path: Optional[str] = None):
    26→        """
    27→        Initialize session manager.
    28→
    29→        Args:
    30→            session_path: Path to session file. Uses config default if None.
    31→        """
    32→        if session_path is None:
    33→            config = get_config()
    34→            session_path = config.get('data.session_path', 'data/.session/maimai_session.json')
    35→
    36→        self.session_path = Path(session_path)
    37→        self._key_path = self.session_path.parent / '.session_key'
    38→        self._fernet: Optional[Fernet] = None
    39→        self._ensure_session_dir()
    40→
    41→    def _ensure_session_dir(self) -> None:
    42→        """Ensure session directory exists with restrictive permissions."""
    43→        session_dir = self.session_path.parent
    44→        session_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    45→        # Restrict directory to owner only (rwx------)
    46→        try:
    47→            os.chmod(session_dir, stat.S_IRWXU)
    48→        except OSError:
    49→            logger.warning("Could not set restrictive permissions on session directory")
    50→
    51→    def _get_fernet(self) -> Fernet:
    52→        """Get or create Fernet encryption instance."""
    53→        if self._fernet is not None:
    54→            return self._fernet
    55→
    56→        if self._key_path.exists():
    57→            key = self._key_path.read_bytes().strip()
    58→        else:
    59→            key = Fernet.generate_key()
    60→            # Write key with owner-only permissions
    61→            fd = os.open(str(self._key_path), os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC, 0o600)
    62→            try:
    63→                os.write(fd, key)
    64→            finally:
    65→                os.close(fd)
    66→
    67→        self._fernet = Fernet(key)
    68→        return self._fernet
    69→
    70→    def _write_secure(self, path: Path, data: bytes) -> None:
    71→        """Write data to file with owner-only permissions (0600)."""
    72→        fd = os.open(str(path), os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC, 0o600)
    73→        try:
    74→            os.write(fd, data)
    75→        finally:
    76→            os.close(fd)
    77→
    78→    def has_saved_session(self) -> bool:
    79→        """
    80→        Check if a saved session exists.
    81→
    82→        Returns:
    83→            True if session file exists
    84→        """
    85→        return self.session_path.exists()
    86→
    87→    async def save(self, context: BrowserContext) -> None:
    88→        """
    89→        Save browser session state.
    90→
    91→        Args:
    92→            context: Playwright browser context
    93→        """
    94→        try:
    95→            # Get storage state (cookies + localStorage)
    96→            storage_state = await context.storage_state()
    97→
    98→            # Add metadata
    99→            session_data = {
   100→                'saved_at': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
   101→                'storage_state': storage_state
   102→            }
   103→
   104→            # Encrypt and write with restrictive permissions
   105→            plaintext = json.dumps(session_data, ensure_ascii=False).encode('utf-8')
   106→            encrypted = self._get_fernet().encrypt(plaintext)
   107→            self._write_secure(self.session_path, encrypted)
   108→
   109→            logger.info(f"Session saved (encrypted) to {self.session_path}")
   110→
   111→        except Exception as e:
   112→            logger.error(f"Failed to save session: {e}")
   113→            raise
   114→
   115→    async def load(self, context: BrowserContext) -> bool:
   116→        """
   117→        Load saved session into browser context.
   118→
   119→        Args:
   120→            context: Playwright browser context
   121→
   122→        Returns:
   123→            True if session was loaded successfully
   124→        """
   125→        if not self.has_saved_session():
   126→            logger.debug("No saved session found")
   127→            return False
   128→
   129→        try:
   130→            encrypted = self.session_path.read_bytes()
   131→            plaintext = self._get_fernet().decrypt(encrypted)
   132→            session_data = json.loads(plaintext.decode('utf-8'))
   133→
   134→            # Check session age (warn if old)
   135→            saved_at = datetime.fromisoformat(session_data.get('saved_at', '2000-01-01'))
   136→            age = datetime.utcnow() - saved_at
   137→
   138→            if age > timedelta(days=7):
   139→                logger.warning(f"Session is {age.days} days old, may be expired")
   140→
   141→            # Apply storage state
   142→            storage_state = session_data.get('storage_state', {})
   143→
   144→            # Add cookies
   145→            cookies = storage_state.get('cookies', [])
   146→            if cookies:
   147→                await context.add_cookies(cookies)
   148→                logger.debug(f"Loaded {len(cookies)} cookies")
   149→
   150→            logger.info("Session loaded successfully")
   151→            return True
   152→
   153→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   154→            logger.error(f"Invalid session file: {e}")
   155→            return False
   156→        except Exception as e:
   157→            logger.error(f"Failed to load session: {e}")
   158→            return False
   159→
   160→    async def is_valid(self, page: Page) -> bool:
   161→        """
   162→        Check if current session is valid (user is logged in).
   163→
   164→        Detects login state by checking for logged-in indicators on page.
   165→
   166→        Args:
   167→            page: Playwright page instance
   168→
   169→        Returns:
   170→            True if session appears valid
   171→        """
   172→        try:
   173→            config = get_config()
   174→
   175→            # Try to find logged-in indicator elements
   176→            logged_in_selector = config.get_selector('login.logged_in_indicator')
   177→
   178→            # Wait briefly for indicator
   179→            try:
   180→                await page.wait_for_selector(
   181→                    logged_in_selector,
   182→                    timeout=5000,
   183→                    state='visible'
   184→                )
   185→                logger.debug("Session is valid - logged in indicator found")
   186→                return True
   187→            except Exception:
   188→                logger.debug("Session invalid - logged in indicator not found")
   189→                return False
   190→
   191→        except Exception as e:
   192→            logger.error(f"Error checking session validity: {e}")
   193→            return False
   194→
   195→    async def clear(self) -> None:
   196→        """Clear saved session and encryption key."""
   197→        if self.session_path.exists():
   198→            self.session_path.unlink()
   199→            logger.info("Session cleared")
   200→
   201→        # Also clear storage state file if exists
   202→        state_path = self.session_path.parent / 'storage_state.json'
   203→        if state_path.exists():
   204→            state_path.unlink()
   205→
   206→        # Remove encryption key so a fresh one is generated next time
   207→        if self._key_path.exists():
   208→            self._key_path.unlink()
   209→        self._fernet = None
   210→
   211→    def get_session_info(self) -> Optional[dict]:
   212→        """
   213→        Get information about saved session.
   214→
   215→        Returns:
   216→            Dict with session metadata or None if no session
   217→        """
   218→        if not self.has_saved_session():
   219→            return None
   220→
   221→        try:
   222→            encrypted = self.session_path.read_bytes()
   223→            plaintext = self._get_fernet().decrypt(encrypted)
   224→            session_data = json.loads(plaintext.decode('utf-8'))
   225→
   226→            saved_at = session_data.get('saved_at')
   227→            storage_state = session_data.get('storage_state', {})
   228→            cookies = storage_state.get('cookies', [])
   229→
   230→            # Calculate age
   231→            if saved_at:
   232→                saved_datetime = datetime.fromisoformat(saved_at)
   233→                age = datetime.utcnow() - saved_datetime
   234→                age_str = f"{age.days}d {age.seconds // 3600}h"
   235→            else:
   236→                age_str = "unknown"
   237→
   238→            return {
   239→                'saved_at': saved_at,
   240→                'age': age_str,
   241→                'cookie_count': len(cookies),
   242→            }
   243→
   244→        except Exception as e:
   245→            logger.error(f"Failed to get session info: {e}")
   246→            return None
   247→
   248→
   249→# Global session manager instance
   250→_session_manager = None
   251→
   252→
   253→def get_session_manager() -> SessionManager:
   254→    """Get global session manager instance."""
   255→    global _session_manager
   256→    if _session_manager is None:
   257→        _session_manager = SessionManager()
   258→    return _session_manager
   259→

1→"""Anti-detection module for browser automation.
     2→
     3→Implements medium-level anti-detection:
     4→- Hide webdriver property
     5→- Mask navigator properties
     6→- Randomize mouse movements
     7→- Human-like scrolling behavior
     8→"""
     9→
    10→import random
    11→
    12→from patchright.async_api import Page
    13→from emunium import EmuniumPlaywright
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→
    17→# JavaScript to inject for hiding automation indicators
    18→STEALTH_JS = """
    19→() => {
    20→    // Hide webdriver property
    21→    Object.defineProperty(navigator, 'webdriver', {
    22→        get: () => undefined
    23→    });
    24→
    25→    // Mask automation-related properties
    26→    Object.defineProperty(navigator, 'plugins', {
    27→        get: () => [
    28→            { name: 'Chrome PDF Plugin', filename: 'internal-pdf-viewer' },
    29→            { name: 'Chrome PDF Viewer', filename: 'mhjfbmdgcfjbbpaeojofohoefgiehjai' },
    30→            { name: 'Native Client', filename: 'internal-nacl-plugin' }
    31→        ]
    32→    });
    33→
    34→    Object.defineProperty(navigator, 'languages', {
    35→        get: () => ['zh-CN', 'zh', 'en-US', 'en']
    36→    });
    37→
    38→    // Override permissions query
    39→    const originalQuery = window.navigator.permissions.query;
    40→    window.navigator.permissions.query = (parameters) => (
    41→        parameters.name === 'notifications' ?
    42→            Promise.resolve({ state: Notification.permission }) :
    43→            originalQuery(parameters)
    44→    );
    45→
    46→    // Hide automation-controlled flag in Chrome
    47→    if (window.chrome) {
    48→        window.chrome.runtime = {
    49→            PlatformOs: { MAC: 'mac', WIN: 'win', ANDROID: 'android', CROS: 'cros', LINUX: 'linux', OPENBSD: 'openbsd' },
    50→            PlatformArch: { ARM: 'arm', X86_32: 'x86-32', X86_64: 'x86-64' },
    51→            PlatformNaclArch: { ARM: 'arm', X86_32: 'x86-32', X86_64: 'x86-64' },
    52→            RequestUpdateCheckStatus: { THROTTLED: 'throttled', NO_UPDATE: 'no_update', UPDATE_AVAILABLE: 'update_available' },
    53→            OnInstalledReason: { INSTALL: 'install', UPDATE: 'update', CHROME_UPDATE: 'chrome_update', SHARED_MODULE_UPDATE: 'shared_module_update' },
    54→            OnRestartRequiredReason: { APP_UPDATE: 'app_update', OS_UPDATE: 'os_update', PERIODIC: 'periodic' }
    55→        };
    56→    }
    57→
    58→}
    59→"""
    60→
    61→
    62→async def apply_stealth_scripts(page: Page) -> None:
    63→    """
    64→    Apply anti-detection scripts to a page.
    65→
    66→    Should be called after page creation and before navigation.
    67→
    68→    Args:
    69→        page: Playwright page instance
    70→    """
    71→    logger.debug("Applying stealth scripts to page")
    72→
    73→    # Add init script that runs on every navigation
    74→    await page.add_init_script(STEALTH_JS)
    75→
    76→    logger.debug("Stealth scripts applied successfully")
    77→
    78→
    79→async def human_scroll(page: Page, direction: str = 'down', distance: int = None) -> None:
    80→    """
    81→    Perform human-like scrolling with variable speed.
    82→
    83→    Args:
    84→        page: Playwright page instance
    85→        direction: 'up' or 'down'
    86→        distance: Scroll distance in pixels (random if None)
    87→    """
    88→    if distance is None:
    89→        distance = random.randint(200, 600)
    90→
    91→    if direction == 'up':
    92→        distance = -distance
    93→
    94→    # Break scroll into smaller chunks
    95→    chunks = random.randint(3, 8)
    96→    chunk_size = distance // chunks
    97→
    98→    for i in range(chunks):
    99→        # Add variance to each chunk
   100→        actual_chunk = chunk_size + random.randint(-20, 20)
   101→
   102→        await page.mouse.wheel(0, actual_chunk)
   103→
   104→        # Variable delay between scroll chunks
   105→        delay = random.uniform(0.05, 0.15)
   106→        await page.wait_for_timeout(delay * 1000)
   107→
   108→    # Small pause after scrolling (human would look at content)
   109→    await page.wait_for_timeout(random.uniform(0.2, 0.5) * 1000)
   110→
   111→
   112→async def human_click(page: Page, selector: str) -> None:
   113→    """
   114→    Click an element with human-like behavior using Emunium.
   115→
   116→    Emunium provides natural mouse movement and click simulation.
   117→
   118→    Args:
   119→        page: Playwright page instance
   120→        selector: CSS selector for element to click
   121→    """
   122→    element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
   123→    if not element:
   124→        raise Exception(f"Element not found: {selector}")
   125→
   126→    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
   127→    await emunium.click_at(element)
   128→
   129→
   130→async def human_type(page: Page, selector: str, text: str) -> None:
   131→    """
   132→    Type text with human-like timing using Emunium.
   133→
   134→    Args:
   135→        page: Playwright page instance
   136→        selector: CSS selector for input element
   137→        text: Text to type
   138→    """
   139→    element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
   140→    if not element:
   141→        raise Exception(f"Element not found: {selector}")
   142→
   143→    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
   144→    await emunium.type_at(element, text)
   145→
   146→
   147→

1→"""Custom exceptions for Maimai Assistant."""
     2→
     3→
     4→class MaimaiAssistantError(Exception):
     5→    """Base exception for all Maimai Assistant errors."""
     6→    pass
     7→
     8→
     9→class BrowserError(MaimaiAssistantError):
    10→    """Raised when browser automation fails."""
    11→    pass
    12→
    13→
    14→class SessionExpiredError(MaimaiAssistantError):

1→"""Database models and setup using SQLAlchemy."""
     2→
     3→from contextlib import contextmanager
     4→from datetime import datetime
     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→from typing import Optional
     7→
     8→from sqlalchemy import (
     9→    Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, JSON,
    10→    ForeignKey, create_engine, Index, CheckConstraint
    11→)
    12→from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
    13→from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config
    17→
    18→Base = declarative_base()
    19→
    20→
    21→class Candidate(Base):
    22→    """Candidate model - stores candidate profile information."""
    23→
    24→    __tablename__ = 'candidates'
    25→
    26→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    27→    maimai_id = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    28→    name = Column(String(100))
    29→    title = Column(String(200))
    30→    company = Column(String(200))

    37→    work_experience = Column(JSON)
    38→    skills = Column(JSON)
    39→

    50→
    51→
    52→class Message(Base):

    57→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)

    59→    template_name = Column(String(100))

    61→    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    62→    retry_count = Column(Integer, default=0)

    66→    # Relationships

    71→
    72→
    73→class SearchHistory(Base):

    78→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    79→    criteria = Column(JSON)  # Search parameters as JSON

    81→    executed_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, index=True)
    82→
    83→    def __repr__(self):
    84→        return f"<SearchHistory(id={self.id}, results={self.results_count}, date='{self.executed_at}')>"
    85→
    86→
    87→class ActionLog(Base):
    88→    """Action log model - tracks all actions for rate limiting."""
    89→
    90→    __tablename__ = 'action_logs'
    91→
    92→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    93→    action_type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)  # 'search', 'view_profile', 'send_message'
    94→    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False)
    95→    details = Column(JSON)  # Additional action details
    96→
    97→    # Composite index for efficient rate limiting queries
    98→    __table_args__ = (
    99→        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
   100→    )
   101→
   102→    def __repr__(self):
   103→        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"
   104→
   105→
   106→class DatabaseManager:
   107→    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""
   108→
   109→    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
   110→        """Initialize database manager."""
   111→        if db_path is None:
   112→            config = get_config()
   113→            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/maimai.db')
   114→
   115→        # Ensure data directory exists
   116→        db_file = Path(db_path)
   117→        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   118→
   119→        # Create engine and session
   120→        self.db_path = db_path
   121→        self.engine = create_engine(f'sqlite:///{db_path}', echo=False)
   122→        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
   123→
   124→        # Auto-create tables and run migrations
   125→        self.init_db()
   126→
   127→        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")
   128→
   129→    def init_db(self):
   130→        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
   131→        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
   132→        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")
   133→
   134→        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
   135→        run_migrations(self.engine)
   136→
   137→    @contextmanager
   138→    def session_scope(self):
   139→        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
   140→        session = self.Session()
   141→        try:
   142→            yield session
   143→            session.commit()
   144→        except Exception:
   145→            session.rollback()
   146→            raise
   147→        finally:
   148→            session.close()
   149→
   150→    def drop_all(self):
   151→        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
   152→        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
   153→        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")
   154→
   155→
   156→# Global database manager instance
   157→_db_manager = None
   158→
   159→
   160→def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
   161→    """Get global database manager instance."""
   162→    global _db_manager
   163→    if _db_manager is None:
   164→        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
   165→    return _db_manager
   166→
   167→
   168→def init_database():
   169→    """Initialize database with all tables."""
   170→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   171→    db_manager.init_db()
   172→

1→"""Lightweight database migration system.
     2→
     3→Tracks schema versions in a dedicated table and applies incremental
     4→migrations on startup.  Each migration is a plain function that
     5→receives a SQLAlchemy connection.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from typing import Callable, List, Tuple
     9→
    10→from sqlalchemy import text, inspect
    11→from loguru import logger
    12→
    13→
    14→# Migration registry: list of (version, description, migration_fn)
    15→# Append new migrations at the end; never reorder or remove existing entries.
    16→_migrations: List[Tuple[int, str, Callable]] = []
    17→
    18→
    19→def migration(version: int, description: str):
    20→    """Decorator to register a migration function."""
    21→    def decorator(fn: Callable):
    22→        _migrations.append((version, description, fn))
    23→        return fn
    24→    return decorator
    25→
    26→
    27→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→#  Built-in migrations
    29→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→
    31→@migration(1, "Initial schema — create core tables if missing")
    32→def _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    33→    """Ensure core tables exist (safe for existing databases)."""
    34→    # SQLAlchemy create_all is called before migrations, so tables should
    35→    # already exist.  This migration just marks the baseline version.
    36→    pass
    37→
    38→
    39→@migration(2, "Add retry_count column to messages table")
    40→def _m002_messages_retry_count(conn):
    41→    inspector = inspect(conn)
    42→    columns = [c["name"] for c in inspector.get_columns("messages")]
    43→    if "retry_count" not in columns:
    44→        conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0"))
    45→
    46→
    47→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→#  Migration runner
    49→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→
    51→def _ensure_version_table(conn) -> None:
    52→    """Create the schema_version table if it doesn't exist."""
    53→    conn.execute(text("""
    54→        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
    55→            version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    56→            description TEXT NOT NULL,
    57→            applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    58→        )
    59→    """))
    60→    conn.commit()
    61→
    62→
    63→def _current_version(conn) -> int:
    64→    """Return highest applied migration version, or 0 if none."""
    65→    result = conn.execute(text("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"))
    66→    row = result.fetchone()
    67→    return row[0] or 0 if row else 0
    68→
    69→
    70→def run_migrations(engine) -> None:
    71→    """Apply all pending migrations against *engine*.
    72→
    73→    Called automatically by DatabaseManager.init_db().
    74→    """
    75→    with engine.connect() as conn:
    76→        _ensure_version_table(conn)
    77→        current = _current_version(conn)
    78→
    79→        pending = [(v, d, fn) for v, d, fn in _migrations if v > current]
    80→        if not pending:
    81→            logger.debug(f"Database schema is up-to-date (version {current})")
    82→            return
    83→
    84→        for version, description, fn in sorted(pending):
    85→            logger.info(f"Applying migration v{version}: {description}")
    86→            fn(conn)
    87→            conn.execute(
    88→                text("INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description) VALUES (:v, :d)"),
    89→                {"v": version, "d": description},
    90→            )
    91→            conn.commit()
    92→
    93→        final = _current_version(conn)
    94→        logger.info(f"Migrations complete — schema version {final}")
    95→

1→"""Authentication module for Maimai login.
     2→
     3→In CDP mode, the user logs in manually in their own Chrome before running
     4→CLI commands. This module verifies login status and provides guidance
     5→if the user is not logged in.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→from typing import Optional
    10→
    11→from patchright.async_api import Page
    12→from loguru import logger
    13→from rich.console import Console
    14→from rich.panel import Panel
    15→
    16→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17→from src.core.session import get_session_manager
    18→from src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    19→from src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError
    20→
    21→console = Console()
    22→
    23→
    24→async def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    25→    """
    26→    Verify login status in CDP-connected Chrome.
    27→
    28→    In CDP mode, the user is expected to already be logged in.
    29→    If not, instructions are printed for manual login in the browser.
    30→
    31→    Args:
    32→        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)
    33→
    34→    Returns:
    35→        True if logged in
    36→    """
    37→    if browser is None:
    38→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    39→
    40→    # Connect to Chrome via CDP
    41→    page = await browser.start()
    42→
    43→    # Check if already logged in
    44→    if await check_login_status(page):
    45→        console.print("[green]Already logged in![/green]")
    46→        # Save session as backup
    47→        await browser.save_session()
    48→        return True
    49→
    50→    # Not logged in — guide the user
    51→    console.print(Panel(
    52→        "[bold]Not Logged In[/bold]\n\n"
    53→        "Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser:\n"
    54→        "1. Go to https://maimai.cn/login in Chrome\n"
    55→        "2. Scan QR code with Maimai app, OR\n"
    56→        "3. Enter phone number and verification code\n\n"
    57→        "[dim]Then run this command again to verify.[/dim]",
    58→        title="Maimai Login",
    59→        border_style="cyan"
    60→    ))
    61→
    62→    # Poll for login (user may log in while we wait)
    63→    max_wait_seconds = 300  # 5 minutes timeout
    64→    poll_interval = 3
    65→    elapsed = 0
    66→
    67→    console.print("[dim]Waiting for login... (timeout: 5 minutes)[/dim]")
    68→
    69→    while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
    70→        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
    71→        elapsed += poll_interval
    72→
    73→        # Check for captcha
    74→        await check_captcha(page)
    75→
    76→        if await check_login_status(page):
    77→            console.print("[green]Login successful![/green]")
    78→            await browser.save_session()
    79→            console.print("[green]Session saved as backup[/green]")
    80→            return True
    81→
    82→    console.print("[red]Login timeout. Please log in manually and try again.[/red]")
    83→    return False
    84→
    85→
    86→async def check_login_status(page: Page) -> bool:
    87→    """
    88→    Check if user is currently logged in by navigating to a protected page
    89→    and observing whether Maimai redirects to the login page.
    90→
    91→    API probes can give false positives (server returns 200 but session is
    92→    actually stale). The redirect-based approach is reliable because Maimai's
    93→    server-side auth check only fires on real page navigations, and it will
    94→    redirect to the login page if the session is invalid.
    95→
    96→    Args:
    97→        page: Playwright page instance
    98→
    99→    Returns:
   100→        True if logged in (no redirect to login page)
   101→    """
   102→    try:
   103→        # Navigate to a protected page that requires authentication.
   104→        # The search center page reliably triggers server-side auth checks.
   105→        protected_url = "https://maimai.cn/web/search_center"
   106→        current_url = page.url
   107→
   108→        # Only navigate if we're not already on a protected maimai page
   109→        if 'maimai.cn' not in current_url or 'login' in current_url:
   110→            await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   111→        else:
   112→            # We're on maimai but need to trigger an auth check.
   113→            # Navigate to the protected page to force the server-side check.
   114→            await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   115→
   116→        # Wait for any redirects to settle (server-side auth redirect happens
   117→        # after initial page load and JS execution)
   118→        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
   119→
   120→        # Check 1: URL-based detection - did we get redirected to login?
   121→        final_url = page.url
   122→        if 'login' in final_url.lower():
   123→            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=redirected_to_login (url={final_url})")
   124→            return False
   125→
   126→        # Check 2: Page content detection - look for login form elements
   127→        # even if URL didn't change (some login overlays don't change the URL)
   128→        is_login_page = await page.evaluate("""
   129→            () => {
   130→                const bodyText = document.body ? document.body.innerText : '';
   131→                const loginIndicators = [
   132→                    '欢迎使用脉脉',
   133→                    '登录/注册',
   134→                    '请输入手机号',
   135→                    '获取验证码',
   136→                    '扫码登录',
   137→                    '手机号登录'
   138→                ];
   139→                for (const indicator of loginIndicators) {
   140→                    if (bodyText.includes(indicator)) {
   141→                        return { is_login: true, matched: indicator };
   142→                    }
   143→                }
   144→
   145→                // Also check for login form elements in DOM
   146→                const loginForm = document.querySelector(
   147→                    'input[type="tel"], input[placeholder*="手机"], ' +
   148→                    '[class*="qrcode"], [class*="scan-login"]'
   149→                );
   150→                if (loginForm) {
   151→                    return { is_login: true, matched: 'login_form_element' };
   152→                }
   153→
   154→                return { is_login: false, matched: null };
   155→            }
   156→        """)
   157→
   158→        if is_login_page.get('is_login', False):
   159→            detail = is_login_page.get('matched', 'unknown')
   160→            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=login_page_detected ({detail})")
   161→            return False
   162→
   163→        logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=True, detail=protected_page_accessible (url={final_url})")
   164→        return True
   165→
   166→    except Exception as e:
   167→        logger.error(f"Error checking login status: {e}")
   168→        return False
   169→
   170→
   171→async def ensure_logged_in(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
   172→    """
   173→    Ensure user is logged in, prompting for login if needed.
   174→
   175→    Args:
   176→        browser: Browser manager instance
   177→
   178→    Returns:
   179→        True if logged in (existing or new login)
   180→
   181→    Raises:
   182→        SessionExpiredError: If login fails
   183→    """
   184→    if browser is None:
   185→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   186→
   187→    # Connect to Chrome via CDP if not already connected
   188→    if not browser.is_running:
   189→        await browser.start()
   190→
   191→    # Check login status
   192→    if browser.page:
   193→        if await check_login_status(browser.page):
   194→            return True
   195→
   196→    # Not logged in — guide user
   197→    console.print("[yellow]Not logged in. Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser.[/yellow]")
   198→
   199→    if await login(browser):
   200→        return True
   201→
   202→    raise SessionExpiredError("Not logged in to Maimai. Please log in manually in Chrome and try again.")
   203→
   204→
   205→async def logout(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> None:
   206→    """
   207→    Log out and clear saved session backup.
   208→
   209→    Args:
   210→        browser: Browser manager instance
   211→    """
   212→    if browser is None:
   213→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   214→
   215→    session_manager = get_session_manager()
   216→
   217→    # Clear saved session backup
   218→    await session_manager.clear()
   219→
   220→    # Disconnect from Chrome (don't close it)
   221→    await browser.close()
   222→
   223→    console.print("[green]Session backup cleared. To fully log out, clear cookies in Chrome.[/green]")
   224→

    59→    location: Optional[str] = None

    61→    experience_years: Optional[str] = None
    62→    education: List[Education] = field(default_factory=list)
    63→    work_experience: List[WorkExperience] = field(default_factory=list)
    64→    skills: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    65→    basic_info: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    66→
    67→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    68→        result = asdict(self)
    69→        # Convert nested dataclasses
    70→        result['education'] = [asdict(e) for e in self.education]
    71→        result['work_experience'] = [asdict(w) for w in self.work_experience]
    72→        return result

    75→async def extract_profile(
    76→    maimai_id: str,
    77→    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None
    78→) -> CandidateDetail:
    79→    """
    80→    Extract detailed profile for a candidate.
    81→
    82→    Args:
    83→        maimai_id: Maimai user ID
    84→        browser: Browser manager instance
    85→
    86→    Returns:
    87→        CandidateDetail with full profile information
    88→
    89→    Raises:
    90→        RateLimitExceededError: If rate limit exceeded
    91→        ExtractionError: If profile extraction fails
    92→    """
    93→    # Validate input
    94→    validate_maimai_id(maimai_id)
    95→
    96→    if browser is None:
    97→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    98→
    99→    config = get_config()
   100→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   101→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   102→
   103→    # Ensure logged in
   104→    await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   105→
   106→    # Check rate limit (waits through min-interval)
   107→    await async_check_rate_limit('view_profile')
   108→
   109→    console.print(f"[cyan]Extracting profile for ID: {maimai_id}...[/cyan]")
   110→
   111→    try:
   112→        page = browser.page
   113→        profile_url = MAIMAI_PROFILE_URL.format(maimai_id=maimai_id)
   114→
   115→        # Navigate to profile
   116→        await retry_navigation(page, profile_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   117→
   118→        # Wait for page load
   119→        await async_random_delay('page_load')
   120→
   121→        # Simulate human behavior - scroll to load content
   122→        await human_scroll(page, 'down', distance=400)
   123→        await async_random_delay('action')
   124→
   125→        # Extract profile data
   126→        profile = await _extract_profile_data(page, maimai_id, profile_url, config)
   127→
   128→        # Log the action
   129→        rate_limiter.log_action('view_profile', {
   130→            'maimai_id': maimai_id,
   131→            'name': profile.name
   132→        })
   133→
   134→        # Update database
   135→        _update_candidate_in_db(db_manager, profile)
   136→
   137→        # Display summary
   138→        _display_profile_summary(profile)
   139→
   140→        return profile
   141→
   142→    except Exception as e:
   143→        logger.error(f"Error extracting profile: {e}")
   144→        await browser.screenshot(f"profile_error_{maimai_id}")
   145→        raise ExtractionError(f"Failed to extract profile for {maimai_id}: {e}")

   229→async def _extract_text(parent, selectors: List[str]) -> Optional[str]:
   230→    """
   231→    Try multiple selectors to extract text content from a parent (Page or Element).
   232→
   233→    Args:
   234→        parent: Playwright Page or ElementHandle to query within
   235→        selectors: List of selectors to try
   236→
   237→    Returns:
   238→        Extracted text or None
   239→    """
   240→    for selector in selectors:
   241→        try:
   242→            elem = await parent.query_selector(selector)
   243→            if elem:
   244→                text = await elem.inner_text()
   245→                text = text.strip()
   246→                if text:
   247→                    return text
   248→        except Exception:
   249→            continue
   250→    return None
   251→
   252→
   253→async def _find_section(page: Page, section_selectors: List[str]) -> Optional[Any]:
   254→    """Find a section element on page using multiple selectors.
   255→
   256→    Args:
   257→        page: Playwright page instance
   258→        section_selectors: List of CSS selectors to try
   259→
   260→    Returns:
   261→        Found element or None
   262→    """
   263→    for selector in section_selectors:
   264→        try:
   265→            section = await page.query_selector(selector)
   266→            if section:
   267→                return section
   268→        except Exception:
   269→            continue
   270→    return None
   271→
   272→
   273→async def _find_items(section, item_selectors: List[str]) -> list:
   274→    """Find items within a section using multiple selectors.
   275→
   276→    Args:
   277→        section: Parent element to query within
   278→        item_selectors: List of CSS selectors to try
   279→
   280→    Returns:
   281→        List of found elements (may be empty)
   282→    """
   283→    for selector in item_selectors:
   284→        items = await section.query_selector_all(selector)
   285→        if items:
   286→            return items
   287→    return []
   288→
   289→
   290→async def _extract_education(page: Page, config) -> List[Education]:
   291→    """Extract education history from profile."""
   292→    education_list = []
   293→
   294→    try:
   295→        section = await _find_section(page, [
   296→            config.get_selector('profile.education_section'),
   297→            '[class*="education"]',
   298→            '[class*="edu-section"]',
   299→            '#education',
   300→        ])
   301→        if not section:
   302→            logger.debug("Education section not found")
   303→            return education_list
   304→
   305→        items = await _find_items(section, [
   306→            config.get_selector('profile.education_item'),
   307→            '[class*="edu-item"]',
   308→            '[class*="education-item"]',
   309→            '.timeline-item',
   310→        ])
   311→
   312→        for item in items:
   313→            try:
   314→                school = await _extract_text(item, [
   315→                    '[class*="school"]', '[class*="name"]', 'h4', '.title'
   316→                ])
   317→                degree = await _extract_text(item, [
   318→                    '[class*="degree"]', '[class*="level"]'
   319→                ])
   320→                major = await _extract_text(item, [
   321→                    '[class*="major"]', '[class*="field"]'
   322→                ])
   323→                date_text = await _extract_text(item, [
   324→                    '[class*="date"]', '[class*="time"]', '.period'
   325→                ])
   326→
   327→                start_year, end_year = _parse_date_range(date_text)
   328→
   329→                if school:
   330→                    education_list.append(Education(
   331→                        school=school,
   332→                        degree=degree,
   333→                        major=major,
   334→                        start_year=start_year,
   335→                        end_year=end_year
   336→                    ))
   337→            except Exception as e:
   338→                logger.debug(f"Error extracting education item: {e}")
   339→                continue
   340→
   341→    except Exception as e:
   342→        logger.debug(f"Error extracting education section: {e}")
   343→
   344→    return education_list

   405→async def _extract_skills(page: Page, config) -> List[str]:
   406→    """Extract skills from profile."""
   407→    skills = []
   408→
   409→    try:
   410→        section = await _find_section(page, [
   411→            config.get_selector('profile.skills_section'),
   412→            '[class*="skills"]',
   413→            '[class*="skill-section"]',
   414→            '#skills',
   415→        ])
   416→        if not section:
   417→            # Try to find skill tags directly on page
   418→            section = page
   419→
   420→        tags = await _find_items(section, [
   421→            config.get_selector('profile.skill_tag'),
   422→            '[class*="skill-tag"]',
   423→            '[class*="skill-item"]',
   424→            '.tag',
   425→        ])
   426→
   427→        for tag in tags:
   428→            try:
   429→                text = await tag.inner_text()
   430→                text = text.strip()
   431→                if text and text not in skills:
   432→                    skills.append(text)
   433→            except Exception:
   434→                continue
   435→
   436→    except Exception as e:
   437→        logger.debug(f"Error extracting skills: {e}")
   438→
   439→    return skills

   443→
   444→def _parse_date_range(date_text: Optional[str]) -> tuple:
   445→    """
   446→    Parse date range string into start and end dates.
   447→
   448→    Args:
   449→        date_text: Date text like "2020-2023" or "2020.01 - 至今"
   450→
   451→    Returns:
   452→        Tuple of (start_date, end_date)
   453→    """
   454→    if not date_text:
   455→        return None, None
   456→
   457→    import re
   458→
   459→    # Common patterns
   460→    patterns = [
   461→        r'(\d{4})[.\-/年]?\s*[-–至到]\s*(\d{4}|至今|现在|present)',
   462→        r'(\d{4})[.\-/](\d{1,2})?\s*[-–至到]\s*(\d{4}|至今|现在|present)',
   463→    ]
   464→
   465→    for pattern in patterns:
   466→        match = re.search(pattern, date_text, re.IGNORECASE)
   467→        if match:
   468→            groups = match.groups()
   469→            start = groups[0]
   470→            end = groups[-1]
   471→            if end in ['至今', '现在', 'present']:
   472→                end = 'present'
   473→            return start, end
   474→
   475→    return None, None
   476→

   478→def _update_candidate_in_db(db_manager, profile: CandidateDetail) -> None:
   479→    """Update candidate record in database with full profile."""
   480→    try:
   481→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   482→            candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(
   483→                maimai_id=profile.maimai_id
   484→            ).first()
   485→
   486→            if candidate:
   487→                candidate.name = profile.name
   488→                candidate.title = profile.title
   489→                candidate.company = profile.company
   490→                candidate.location = profile.location
   491→                candidate.profile_url = profile.profile_url
   492→                candidate.basic_info = profile.basic_info
   493→                candidate.education = [asdict(e) for e in profile.education]
   494→                candidate.work_experience = [asdict(w) for w in profile.work_experience]
   495→                candidate.skills = profile.skills
   496→            else:
   497→                candidate = Candidate(
   498→                    maimai_id=profile.maimai_id,
   499→                    name=profile.name,
   500→                    title=profile.title,
   501→                    company=profile.company,
   502→                    location=profile.location,
   503→                    profile_url=profile.profile_url,
   504→                    basic_info=profile.basic_info,
   505→                    education=[asdict(e) for e in profile.education],
   506→                    work_experience=[asdict(w) for w in profile.work_experience],
   507→                    skills=profile.skills
   508→                )
   509→                session.add(candidate)
   510→
   511→            logger.debug(f"Updated candidate {profile.maimai_id} in database")
   512→
   513→    except Exception as e:
   514→        logger.error(f"Failed to update candidate in database: {e}")
   515→
   516→
   517→def _display_profile_summary(profile: CandidateDetail) -> None:
   518→    """Display profile summary in terminal."""
   519→    edu_str = ""
   520→    if profile.education:
   521→        edu = profile.education[0]
   522→        edu_str = f"\n  Education: {edu.school}"
   523→        if edu.degree:
   524→            edu_str += f" ({edu.degree})"
   525→
   526→    work_str = ""
   527→    if profile.work_experience:
   528→        work = profile.work_experience[0]
   529→        work_str = f"\n  Experience: {work.title} @ {work.company}"
   530→
   531→    skills_str = ""
   532→    if profile.skills:
   533→        skills_str = f"\n  Skills: {', '.join(profile.skills[:5])}"
   534→        if len(profile.skills) > 5:
   535→            skills_str += f" (+{len(profile.skills) - 5} more)"
   536→
   537→    console.print(Panel(
   538→        f"[bold]{profile.name}[/bold]\n"
   539→        f"  {profile.title} @ {profile.company}"
   540→        f"{edu_str}{work_str}{skills_str}",
   541→        title=f"Profile: {profile.maimai_id}",
   542→        border_style="green"
   543→    ))

    52→def _is_non_rd_title(title: str) -> bool:
    53→    """Check if a title indicates a non-R&D role.
    54→
    55→    Strict mode: if any non-R&D keyword appears, filter out regardless of
    56→    whether R&D keywords are also present (e.g. "技术产品经理" is filtered).
    57→    Returns False (keep) only if title is empty or contains no non-R&D keywords.
    58→    """
    59→    if not title:
    60→        return False
    61→
    62→    # Strict: any non-R&D keyword present → filter out
    63→    if any(kw in title for kw in NON_RD_KEYWORDS):
    64→        return True
    65→
    66→    return False

    70→class CandidateBasic:
    71→    """Basic candidate information from search results."""
    72→    maimai_id: str

    74→    title: str
    75→    company: str
